Since the area of the canvas = 551 m2 and area of the canvas lost in wastage is 1 m2, therefore the area of canvas available for making the tent is (551 - 1) m2 = 550 m2
Now, the surface area of the tent = 550 m2 and the required base radius of the conical tent = 7 m
Therefore, curved surface area of tent = 550 m2
That is, {tex}\pi rl{/tex} = 550
or, {tex}\frac { 22 } { 7 }{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} l = 550
l = {tex}\frac { 550 } { 22 }{/tex}m = 25 m
Now, l2 = r2 + h2
Therefore, h = {tex}\sqrt { l ^ { 2 } - r ^ { 2 } } = \sqrt { 25 ^ { 2 } - 7 ^ { 2 } } m = \sqrt { 625 - 49 m } = \sqrt { 576 m }{/tex}
= 24 m
So, the volume of the conical tent = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 3 }{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 3 } \times \frac { 22 } { 7 }{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 24m3 = 1232 m3

Q 10

Find the volume of a sphere of radius 11.2 cm.

Solution

We know that volume of sphere= {tex}\frac 43 \pi r^3{/tex}
= {tex}\frac 43 \times \frac {22}7 \times 11.2 \times 11.2 \times 11.2 cm^3{/tex} = 5887.32 cm3

Q 11

A shot-putt is a metallic sphere of radius 4.9 cm. If the density of the metal is 7.8 g per cm3, find the mass of the shot-put.

Solution

Since the shot-putt is a solid sphere made of metal and its mass is equal to the product of its volume and density, we need to find the volume of the sphere.
Now, volume of the sphere is given by= {tex}\frac 43 \pi r^3{/tex}
= {tex}\frac 43 \times \frac {22}7 \times 4.9 \times 4.9 \times 4.9 cm^3{/tex}
= 493 cm3 (nearly)
Further, mass of 1 cm3 of metal is 7.8 g.
Therefore, mass of the shot-putt will be = 7.8 {tex}\times{/tex} 493 g
= 3845.44 g = 3.85 kg (nearly)

Q 12

A hemispherical bowl has a radius of 3.5 cm. What would be the volume of water it would contain?

Solution

The volume of water the bowl contain = {tex}\frac{2}{3}\pi {{r}^{3}}{/tex}
Radius = 3.5 cm
Then volume = {tex}\frac{2}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times {{\left( 3.5 \right)}^{3}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times 3.5\times 3.5\times 3.5{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{2}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times \frac{35}{10}\times \frac{35}{10}\times \frac{35}{10}{/tex}
= 89.8 cm3

sections.label: Exercise 11.1
sections.html:
Q 1

Diameter of the base of a cone is 10.5 cm and its slant height is 10 cm. Find its curved surface area.

Solution

Diameter = 10.5 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Radius (r) = {tex}\frac{10.5}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{21}{4}{/tex} cm
Slant height of cone (l) = 10 cm
Now we have, Curved surface area of cone = {tex}\pi rl{/tex} = {tex}\frac{22}{7}\times \frac{21}{4}\times 10{/tex}
= 165 cm2

Q 2

Find the total surface area of a cone, if its slant height is 21 m and diameter of its base is 24 m.

Solution

Slant height (l) = 21 m
Diameter of base = 24 m
∴ Radius of base (r) = {tex}24\over2{/tex}m = 12 m
∴ Total curved surface area of the cone ={tex}\pi r(l+r){/tex}
= {tex}22\over7{/tex}× 12 × (21 + 12)
={tex}{22\over7}\times12\times33={8712\over7}{/tex}
={tex}1244{4\over7}\ m^2{/tex}

Q 3

Curved surface area of a cone is 308 cm2 and its slant height is 14 cm. Find the radius of the base.

Solution

Slant height of cone = 14 cm
Let radius of circular end of cone be r. 
Curved surface area of cone = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
308 cm2 = ({tex}\frac { 22 } { 7 }{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r {tex}\times{/tex} 14) cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\left( \frac { 308 } { 44 } \right){/tex} cm = 7 cm
Thus, the radius of circular end of the cone is 7 cm.

Q 4

Curved surface area of a cone is 308 cm2 and its slant height is 14 cm. Find total surface area of the cone.

Solution

 Total surface area of cone = Curved surface Area of cone + Area of base
= {tex}\pi rl{/tex} + {tex}\pi r{/tex}2
= [308 + {tex}\frac { 22 } { 7 }{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (7)2] cm2
= 462 cm2
Thus, the total surface area of the cone is 462 cm2.

Q 5

A conical tent is 10 m high and the radius of its base is 24 m. Find

  1. Slant height of the tent.
  2. Cost of the canvas required to make the tent, if the cost of 1 m2 canvas is ₹ 70.
Solution
  1. h = 10 m, r = 24 m
    {tex}l = { \sqrt{r^2+h^2}}={\sqrt{(24)^2+(10)^2}}{/tex}
    {tex}= {\sqrt{576+100}}={\sqrt{676}}{/tex}
    = 26 m
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} the slant height of the tent is 26 m.
  2. Curved surface area of the tent = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
    {tex}={22\over7}\times24\times26\ m^2{/tex}
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of the canvas required to make the tent, if the cost of 1 m2 canvas is ₹ 70.
    {tex}={22\over7}\times24\times26\times70{/tex} = ₹ 137280
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} The cost of the canvas is ₹ 137280.
Q 6

What length of tarpaulin 3 m wide will be required to make conical tent of height 8 m and base radius 6 m? Assume that the extra length of material that will be required for stitching margins and wastage in cutting is approximately 20 cm. {tex}\left( \text{Use }\pi \text{ = 3}\text{.14} \right){/tex}

Solution

Height of the conical tent {tex}\left( h \right){/tex} = 8 m and Radius of the conical tent {tex}\left( r \right){/tex} = 6 m
Slant height of the tent {tex}\left( l \right)=\sqrt{{{r}^{2}}+{{h}^{2}}}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{{{\left( 6 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( 8 \right)}^{2}}}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{36+64}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{100}{/tex}
= 10 m
Area of tarpaulin = Curved surface area of tent = {tex}\pi rl{/tex} {tex}=3.14\times 6\times 10=188.4\text{ }{{m}^{2}}{/tex}
Width of tarpaulin = 3 m
Let Length of tarpaulin = L
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Area of tarpaulin = {tex}Length\times Breadth\text{ }=\text{ }L\times 3{/tex} = 3L
Now According to question, 3L = 188.4
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} L = 188.4/3 = 62.8 m
The extra length of the material required for stitching margins and cutting is 20 cm = 0.2 m.
So the total length of tarpaulin bought is (62.8 + 0.2) m = 63 m

Q 7

The slant height and base diameter of a conical tomb are 25 m and 14 m respectively. Find the cost of white washing its curved surface at the rate of ₹ 210 per 100 m2.

Solution

Slant height (l) = 25 m
Base diameter (d) = 14 m
∴ Base radius (r) = {tex}14\over2{/tex}m = 7 m
∴ Curved surface area of the tomb = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
= {tex}22\over7{/tex}× 7 × 25 = 550 m2
∴ Cost of white-washing the curved surface of the tomb at the rate of Rs. 210 per 100 m2
= {tex}210\over100{/tex} × 550 = Rs. 1155.

Q 8

A Joker's cap is in the form of a right circular cone of base radius 7 cm and height 24 cm. Find the area of the sheet required to make 10 such caps.

Solution

Radius of cap (r) = 7 cm, Height of cap (h) = 24 cm
Slant height of the cone (l) = {tex}\sqrt{{{r}^{2}}+{{h}^{2}}}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{{{\left( 7 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( 24 \right)}^{2}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{49+576}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{625}{/tex}
= 25 cm
Area of sheet required to make a cap = CSA of cone = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{22}{7}\times 7\times 25{/tex} 
{tex}=\text{ }550\text{ }c{{m}^{2}}{/tex}=550 cm2
{tex}\therefore{/tex}  Area of sheet required to make 10 caps =10 {tex}\times{/tex} 550 = 5500 cm2

Q 9

A bus stop is barricaded from the remaining part of the road, by using 50 hollow cones made of recycled cardboard. Each cone has a base diameter of 40 cm and height 1 m. If the outer side of each of the cones is to be painted and the cost of painting is ₹ 12 per m2, what will be the cost of painting all these cones? (Use {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3.14 and take {tex}\sqrt {1.04}{/tex} = 1.02)

Solution

Diameter of cone = 40 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Radius of cone (r) = {tex}\frac{40}{2}{/tex}
= 20 cm
= {tex}\frac{20}{100}{/tex}m
= 0.2 m
Height of cone (h) = 1 m
Slant height of cone {tex}\left( l \right)=\sqrt{{{r}^{2}}+{{h}^{2}}}{/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{{{\left( 0.2 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( 1 \right)}^{2}}}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{1.04}{/tex} m
Curved surface area of cone = {tex}\pi rl{/tex}
= {tex}3.14\times 0.2\times \sqrt{1.04}{/tex}
= 0.64056 m2
{tex}\because{/tex} Cost of painting 1m2 of a cone = Rs.12
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of painting 0.64056m2 of a cone = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.64056 = Rs. 7.68672
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Cost of painting of 50 such cones = 50 {tex}\times{/tex} 7.68672 = Rs. 384.34 (approx.)

sections.label: Exercise 11.2
sections.html:
Q 1

Find the surface area of a sphere of radius 10.5 cm.

Solution

r = 10.5 cm
Surface area of a sphere {tex}=4\pi r^2{/tex}
= {tex}4\times{22\over7}\times(10.5)^2{/tex}
= {tex}4\times22\times10.5\times1.5{/tex}
= 1386 cm2

Q 2

Find the surface area of a sphere of radius 5.6 cm.

Solution

r = 5.6 cm
Surface area of a sphere = {tex}4\pi {r^2}{/tex}
{tex} = 4 \times \frac{{22}}{7} \times {(5.6)^2} = 394.24\;c{m^2}{/tex}

Q 3

Find the surface area of a sphere of radius 14 cm.

Solution

r = 14 cm
Surface area of a sphere ={tex}4\pi r^2{/tex}
{tex}=4\times{22\over7}\times(14)^2{/tex}
{tex}=4\times22\times17\times2{/tex}
= 2464 cm2

Q 4

Find the surface area of a sphere of diameter 14 cm.

Solution

Diameter = 14 cm
∴ Radius (r) = {tex}14\over2{/tex} cm = 7 cm
Surface area of sphere = {tex}4\pi r^2{/tex}
= 4×{tex}22\over7{/tex} × (7)2 = 616 cm2

Q 5

Find the surface area of a sphere of diameter 21 cm.

Solution

Diameter = 21 cm
∴ Radius (r) = {tex}21\over2{/tex} cm
Surface area of sphere ={tex}4\pi r^2{/tex}
= {tex}4\times{22\over7}\times({21\over2})^2{/tex}

= {tex}4\times{22\over7}\times{21\over2}\times{21\over2}{/tex}

=1386 cm2

Q 6

Find the surface area of a sphere of diameter 3.5 m.

Solution

Diameter = 3.5 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius (r) = {tex}3.5\over2{/tex} m = 1.75 m
Surface area of sphere = {tex}4\pi r^2{/tex}
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}22\over7{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (1.75)2 = 38.5 m2

Q 7

Find the total surface area of a hemisphere of radius 10 cm. (Use {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3.14)

Solution

r = 10 cm
Total surface area of the hemisphere = {tex}3\pi r^2{/tex}
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3.14 {tex}\times{/tex} (10)2 = 942 cm2

Q 8

The radius of a spherical balloon increases from 7 cm to 14 cm as air is being pumped into it. Find the ratio of surface areas of the balloon in the two cases.

Solution

Case I : r = 7 cm
Surface area = {tex}4\pi r^2{/tex}
= 4 × {tex}22\over7{/tex} × (7)2 = 616 cm2
Case II : r = 14 cm
Surface area = {tex}4\pi r^2{/tex}
= 4 ×{tex}22\over7{/tex} × (14)2 = 2464 cm2
∴ Ratio of surface area of the balloon = 616 : 2464 

={tex}1:4{/tex}

Q 9

A hemispherical bowl made of brass has inner diameter 10.5 cm. Find the cost of tin-plating it on the inside at the rate of ₹ 16 per 100 cm2.

Solution

Inner diameter of bowl = 10.5 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Inner radius of bowl {tex}\left( r \right)=\frac{10.5}{2}{/tex} = 5.25 cm
Now, Inner surface area of bowl = {tex}2\pi {{r}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=2\times \frac{22}{7}\times 5.25\times 5.25{/tex}
{tex}=2\times \frac{22}{7}\times \frac{21}{4}\times \frac{21}{4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{693}{4}c{{m}^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}\because {/tex} Cost of tin-plating per{tex} 100\text{ }c{{m}^{2}}{/tex} = ₹ 16
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Cost of tin-plating per{tex} 1\text{ }c{{m}^{2}}=\frac{16}{100}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Cost of tin-plating per {tex}\frac{693}{4}c{{m}^{2}}=\frac{16}{100}\times \frac{693}{4}{/tex} = ₹ 27.72

Q 10

Find the radius of a sphere whose surface area is 154 cm2.

Solution

Let the radius of the sphere be r cm.
Surface area = 154 cm2
⇒ {tex}4\pi r^2{/tex} = 154
⇒ {tex}4\times{22\over7}\times r^2=154{/tex}
⇒ {tex}r^2={{154\times7}\over4\times22}={49\over4}{/tex}
⇒ r = {tex}{\sqrt{49\over4}}={7\over2}{/tex}
⇒ r = 3.5 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} the radius of the sphere is 3.5 cm.

Q 11

The diameter of the moon is approximately one fourth of the diameter of the earth. Find the ratio of their surface areas.

Solution

Let diameter of Earth = x
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius of Earth {tex}\left( r \right)=\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Surface area of Earth = {tex}4\pi {{r}^{2}}{/tex}= {tex}4\pi \times \frac{x}{2}\times \frac{x}{2}=\pi {{x}^{2}}{/tex}
 
Now, Diameter of Moon = {tex}\frac{1}{4}th{/tex} of diameter of Earth = {tex}\frac{x}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius of Moon {tex}\left( r \right){/tex} = {tex}\frac{x}{8}{/tex}
Surface area of Moon = {tex}4\pi {{r}^{2}}{/tex} = {tex}4\pi \times \frac{x}{8}\times \frac{x}{8}=\frac{\pi {{x}^{2}}}{16}{/tex}
Now, Ratio = {tex}\frac{\text{Surface area of Moon}}{\text{Surface area of Earth}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{\frac{\pi {{x}^{2}}}{16}}{\pi {{x}^{2}}}{/tex}={tex}\frac{\pi {{x}^{2}}}{16}\times \frac{1}{\pi {{x}^{2}}}{/tex}={tex}\frac{1}{16}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Required ratio = 1 : 16

Q 12

A hemispherical bowl is made of steel, 0.25 cm thick. The inner radius of the bowl is 5 cm. Find the outer curved surface area of the bowl.
 

Solution

Inner radius of bowl {tex}\left( r \right){/tex} = 5 cm
Thickness of steel {tex}\left( t \right){/tex} = 0.25 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Outer radius of bowl (R) = {tex}r+t{/tex} = 5 +0.25 = 5.25 cm
{tex}\therefore {/tex} Outer curved surface area of bowl = {tex}2\pi {{\text{R}}^{2}}{/tex}= {tex}2\times \frac{22}{7}\times 5.25\times 5.25{/tex}
= {tex}2\times \frac{22}{7}\times \frac{21}{4}\times \frac{21}{4}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{693}{4}{/tex}
 {tex}=\text{ }173.25\text{ }c{{m}^{2}}{/tex}

Q 13

A right circular cylinder just encloses a sphere of radius r (see Figure). Find

  1. surface area of the sphere.
  2. curved surface area of the cylinder.
  3. ratio of the areas obtained in (i) and (ii).
Solution
  1. Surface area of the sphere = {tex}4\pi r^2{/tex}
  2. For cylinder
    Radius of the base = r
    Height = 2r
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Curved surface area of the cylinder = {tex}2\pi rh=2\pi (r)(2r)=4\pi r^2{/tex}
  3. Ratio of the areas obtained in (i) and (ii)
    {tex}={{surface\ area\ of\ the\ sphere}\over{curved\ surface\ area\ of\ the\ cylinder}}{/tex} 
    {tex}={{4\pi r^2}\over{4\pi r^2}}={1\over1}=1:1{/tex}

sections.label: Exercise 11.3
sections.html:
Q 1

Find the volume of the right circular cone with radius 6 cm and height 7 cm.

Solution

r = 6 cm, h = 7 cm
∴ Volume of the right circular cone = {tex}{1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex}
{tex}={1\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(6)^2\times7{/tex} = 264 cm3

Q 2

Find the volume of the right circular cone with radius 3.5 cm and height 12 cm.

Solution

r = 3.5 cm, h = 12 cm.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume of the right circular cone {tex}={1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex}
{tex}={1\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(3.5)^2\times12{/tex} = 154 cm3

Q 3

Find the capacity in litres of a conical vessel with radius 7 cm, slant height 25 cm.

Solution

r = 7 cm, l = 25 cm.
r2 + h2 = l2
⇒ (7)2 + h2 = (25)2
⇒ h2 = (25)2 – (7)2
⇒ h2 = 625 – 49
⇒ h2 = 576
⇒ h = {tex}\sqrt{576}{/tex}
⇒ h = 24 cm
∴ Capacity {tex}={1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex}
{tex}={1\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(7)^2\times24{/tex}
= 1232 cm3 = 1.232 l

Q 4

Find the capacity in litres of a conical vessel with height 12 cm, slant height 13 cm.

Solution

12 cm, l = 13 cm.
r2 + h2 = l2
⇒ r2 + (12)2 = (13)2
⇒ r2 + 144 = 169
⇒ r2 = 169 – 144
⇒ r2 = 25
⇒ r = {tex}\sqrt{25}{/tex}
⇒ r = 5 cm
∴ Capacity {tex}={1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex}
{tex}={1\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(5)^2\times12{/tex}
{tex}={2200\over7}\ cm^3={2200\over7000}l{/tex}
{tex}={11\over35}l{/tex}

Q 5

The height of a cone is 15 cm. If its volume is 1570 cm3, find the radius of the base. (Use {tex}\pi{/tex} = 3.14)

Solution

Let the radius of the base of the cone be r cm.
h = 15 cm, Volume = 1570 cm3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex} = 1570
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}1\over3{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 3.14 {tex}\times{/tex} r2 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 1570
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}r^2={{1570\times3}\over3.14\times15}{/tex} ⇒ {tex}r^2=100{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}r=\sqrt{100}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 10 cm.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} the radius of the base of the cone is 10 cm.

Q 6

If the volume of a right circular cone of height 9 cm is 48{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3, find the diameter of its base.

Solution

Let the radius of the base of the right circular cone be r cm.
h = 9 cm, volume = 48{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3
⇒{tex}{1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex} = 48{tex}\pi{/tex}
⇒{tex}{1\over3}r^2h=48{/tex} 
⇒ {tex}{1\over3}\times r^2\times9=48{/tex}
⇒ r2 = {tex}{48\times3}\over9{/tex}
⇒ r2 = 16 ⇒ r = {tex}\sqrt{16}{/tex} = 4 cm
⇒ 2r = 2(4) = 8 cm.
∴ the diameter of the base of the right circular cone is 8 cm.

Q 7

A conical pit of top diameter 3.5 m is 12 m deep. What is its capacity in kilolitres?

Solution

For conical pit : Diameter = 3.5 m.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius (r) = {tex}{3.5}\over2{/tex}m = 1.75 m
Depth (h) = 12 m
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Capacity of the conical pit = {tex}{1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex}
{tex}{1\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(1.75)^2\times12\ m^3{/tex}
= 38.5 m3 = 38.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 1000 l
= 38.5 kl.

Q 8

The volume of a right circular cone is 9856 cm3. If the diameter of the base is 28 cm, find

  1. Height of the cone
  2. Slant height of the cone
  3. Curved surface area of the cone.
Solution
  1. Diameter of cone = 28 cm
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius of cone = 14 cm
    Volume of cone = {tex}9856\text{ }c{{m}^{3}}{/tex}

     
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{3}\pi {{r}^{2}}h{/tex} = 9856
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times 14\times 14\times h=9856{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}h=\frac{9856\times 3\times 7}{22\times 14\times 14}{/tex} = 48 cm
  2. Slant height of cone {tex}\left( l \right)=\sqrt{{{r}^{2}}+{{h}^{2}}}{/tex}
    = {tex}\sqrt{{{\left( 14 \right)}^{2}}+{{\left( 48 \right)}^{2}}}{/tex}
    = {tex}\sqrt{196+2304}{/tex}
    = {tex}\sqrt{2500}{/tex} = 50 cm
  3. Curved surface area of cone = {tex}\pi rl=\frac{22}{7}\times 14\times 50{/tex} = {tex}2200\text{ }c{{m}^{2}}{/tex}
Q 9

A right triangle ABC with sides 5 cm, 12 cm and 13 cm is revolved about the side 12 cm. Find the volume of the solid so obtained.

Solution


The solid obtained will be a right circular cone whose radius of the base is 5 cm. and height is 12 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} r = 5 cm, h = 12 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume = {tex}{1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex}
{tex}{1\over3}\times\pi \times (5)^2\times12\ cm^3{/tex}
= 100{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3 
The volume of the solid so obtained is 100{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3

Q 10

A right triangle ABC with sides 5 cm, 12 cm, and 13 cm is revolved about the side 12 cm. Find the volume of the solid so obtained. If the triangle ABC is revolved about side 5 cm, then find the volume of the solid so obtained. Find also the ratio of the volumes of the two solids obtained.

Solution

Let ABC be a right triangle with AB = 12 cm, BC = 5 cm and AC = 13 cm.
When this triangle is revolved about AB, it forms a right circular cone of radius = BC = 5 cm and height AB = 12 cm.

{tex}\therefore{/tex} V1 = Volume of the solid formed = Volume of the cone of radius 5 cm and height 12 cm
 {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 3 } \times \pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 cm3 = 100 {tex}\pi{/tex} cm3
When triangle ABC is revolved about BC, it forms a right circular cone of radius AB = 12 cm and height BC = 5 cm.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} V2 = Volume of the solid formed
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} V2 = Volume of the cone of radius 12 cm and height 5 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} V2 {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 3 } \times \pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 cm3 = 240 {tex}\pi{/tex} cm3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Required ratio = V1 : V2 = 100{tex}\pi{/tex} : 240 {tex}\pi{/tex} = 5 : 12

Q 11

A heap of wheat is in the form of a cone whose diameter is 10.5 m and height is 3 m. Find its volume. The heap is to be covered by canvas to protect it from rain. Find the area of the canvas required.

Solution

For heap of wheat
Diameter = 10.5 m
∴ Radius (r) = {tex}10.5\over2{/tex} cm = 5.25 m
Height (h) = 3 m
∴ Volume = {tex}{1\over3}\pi r^2h{/tex}
{tex}={1\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(5.25)^2\times3{/tex}
= 86.625 m3
Slant height,  {tex}l=\sqrt{r^2+h^2}{/tex}
{tex}= \sqrt{(5.25)^2+(3)^2}=\sqrt{27.5625+9}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{36.5625}{/tex} = 6.05 m
∴ Curved surface area ={tex}\pi{/tex}rl
= {tex}22\over7{/tex} × 5.25 × 6.05 = 99.825 m2
∴ The area of the canvas required is 99.825 m2

sections.label: Exercise 11.4
sections.html:
Q 1

Find the volume of a sphere whose radius is 7 cm.

Solution

r = 7 cm
∴ Volume = {tex}{4\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
{tex}={4\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(7)^3={4312\over3}\ cm^3=1437{1\over3}\ cm^3{/tex}

Q 2

Find the volume of a sphere whose radius is 0.63 m.

Solution

r = 0.63 m
∴ volume ={tex}{4\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
{tex}={4\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(0.63)^3=1.05\ m^3{/tex}

Q 3

Find the amount of water displaced by a solid spherical ball of diameter 28 cm.

Solution

Diameter = 28 cm
∴ Radius (r) ={tex}28\over2{/tex} cm = 14 cm
∴ Amount of water displaced =  Volume of solid spherical ball = {tex}{4\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
{tex}={4\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(14)^3={34496\over3}\ cm^3=11498{2\over3}\ cm^3{/tex}

Q 4

Find the amount of water displaced by a solid spherical ball of diameter 0.21 m.

Solution

Diameter = 0.21 m
∴ Radius (r) = {tex}{0.21}\over2{/tex}m = 0.105 m
∴ Amount of water displaced = {tex}{4\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
{tex}={4\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(0.105)^3=0.004851\ m^3{/tex}

Q 5

The diameter of a metallic ball is 4.2 cm. What is the mass of the ball, if the density of the metal is 8.9 g per cm3?

Solution

Diameter of metallic ball = 4.2 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius of metallic ball {tex}\left( r \right){/tex}={tex}\frac{4.2}{2}{/tex} = 2.1 cm
Volume of metallic ball = {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {{r}^{3}}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times 2.1\times 2.1\times 2.1{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times \frac{21}{10}\times \frac{21}{10}\times \frac{21}{10} =38.808 c{{m}^{3}}{/tex}
Density of metal = 8.9 g per cm3
 Density x Volume = Mass
{tex}\because{/tex} Mass of 1 cm3 = 8.9 g
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Mass of 38.808 cm3 4 = 8.9 {tex}\times{/tex} 38.808 = 345.3912 g = 345.39 g (approx.)

Q 6

The diameter of the moon is approximately one-fourth of the diameter of the earth. What fraction of the volume of the earth is the volume of the moon?

Solution

Let diameter of earth be {tex}x{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius of earth {tex}\left( r \right)=\frac{x}{2}{/tex}
Now, Volume of earth = {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {{r}^{3}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} Earth is considered to be a sphere]
= {tex}\frac{4}{3}\times \pi \times \frac{x}{2}\times \frac{x}{2}\times \frac{x}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{8}\times \frac{4}{3}\pi {{x}^{3}}{/tex} ………..(i)
According to question, Diameter of moon = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\times{/tex} Diameter of earth = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\times x=\frac{x}{4}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius of moon (R) = {tex}\frac{x}{8}{/tex}
Now, Volume of Moon = {tex}\frac{4}{3}\pi {{\text{R}}^{3}}{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} Moon is considered to be a sphere]
= {tex}\frac{4}{3}\times \pi \times \frac{x}{8}\times \frac{x}{8}\times \frac{x}{8}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{512}\times \frac{4}{3}\pi {{x}^{3}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{64}\times \left[ \frac{1}{8}\times \frac{4}{3}\pi {{x}^{3}} \right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{64}\times{/tex} Volume of Earth [From eq. (i)]
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume of moon is {tex}\frac{1}{64}th{/tex} the volume of earth.

Q 7

How many litres of milk can a hemispherical bowl of diameter 10.5 cm hold?

Solution

Diameter = 10.5 cm.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius (r) ={tex}10.5\over2{/tex}=5.25 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Amount of milk = {tex}{2\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
{tex}={2\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(5.25)^3\ cm^3{/tex}
= 303 cm3 = 0.303 l

Q 8

A hemispherical tank is made up of an iron sheet 1 cm thick. If the inner radius is 1 m, then find the volume of the iron used to make the tank.

Solution

Inner radius of hemispherical tank (r) = 1 m = 100 cm
Thickness of sheet = 1 cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Outer radius of hemispherical tank (R) = 100 + 1 = 101 cm
Volume of iron of hemisphere = {tex}\frac{2}{3}\pi{/tex} [R3 - r3] cm2
= {tex}\frac{2}{3}\times \frac{22}{7}\times \left[ {{\left( 101 \right)}^{3}}-{{\left( 100 \right)}^{3}} \right]{/tex} cm2
= {tex}\frac{44}{21}{/tex} [1030301 - 1000000] cm2
=  0.06348 m2

Q 9

Find the volume of a sphere whose surface area is 154 cm2.

Solution

Let the radius of the sphere be r cm.
Surface area = 154 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow\ 4\pi r^2{/tex} = 154
{tex}\Rightarrow\ 4\times{22\over7}\times r^2=154{/tex}
 {tex}\Rightarrow\ r^2={154\times7\over4\times22}\Rightarrow {r^2={49\over4} }{/tex} 
 {tex}\Rightarrow r=\sqrt{49\over4}\Rightarrow r={7\over2}{/tex} cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex}Volume of the sphere ={tex}{4\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
{tex}={4\over3}\times{22\over7}\times({7\over2})^2={539\over3}\ cm^3{/tex}
{tex}=179{2\over3}\ cm^3{/tex}

Q 10

A dome of a building is in the form of a hemisphere. From inside, it was white-washed at the cost of ₹ 4989.60. If the cost of white-washing is ₹ 20 per square metre, find the

  1. inside surface area of the dome.
  2. Volume of the air inside the dome.
Solution
  1. Inside surface area of the dome {tex}={4989.60\over20}{/tex} = 249.48 m2
  2. Let the radius of the hemisphere be r m.
    Inside surface area = 249.48 m2
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\pi {/tex}r2 = 249.48
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}22\over7{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} r2 = 249.48
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = {tex}{249.48\times7}\over2\times22{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r2 = 39.69
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = {tex}\sqrt{39.69}{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} r = 6.3 m
    {tex}\therefore{/tex} Volume of the air inside the dome = {tex}{2\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
    {tex}={2\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(6.3)^3{/tex} = 523.9 m3
Q 11

Twenty-seven solid iron spheres, each of radius r and surface area S are melted to form a sphere with surface area S'. Find the

  1. radius r' of the new sphere,
  2. ratio of S and S'.
Solution

Volume of 27 solid sphere, each of radius, {tex}r = 27{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex}{tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}{tex}r^3 = 36{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}{tex}r^3 {/tex}
According to the question,
Volume of sphere of radius r' = Volume of 27 solid spheres
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}{tex}(r')^3 = 36{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}{tex}r^3{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(r')^3 = 27r^3 = (3r)^3{/tex}
 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}r' = 3r{/tex}W
We have,
S' = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r'2 = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}(3r)2 = 36{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\frac{S}{S'}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4 \pi r^2}{36 \pi r^2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{9}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}S : S' = 1 : 9.{/tex}

Q 12

A capsule of medicine is in the shape of a sphere of diameter 3.5 mm. How much medicine (in mm3) is needed to fill this capsule?

Solution

Diameter of the capsule = 3.5 mm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Radius of the capsule (r) = {tex}3.5\over2{/tex} mm = 1.75 mm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Capacity of the capsule = {tex}{4\over3}\pi r^3{/tex}
{tex}={4\over3}\times{22\over7}\times(1.75)^3\ mm^3{/tex}
= 22.46 mm3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} 22.46 mm3 of medicine is needed to fill this capsule.

Q 1

In a particular section of Class IX, 40 students were asked about the months of their birth and the following graph was prepared for the data so obtained:

Observe the bar graph given above and answer the following questions:

  1. How many students were born in the month of November?
  2. In which month were the maximum number of students born?
Solution

from the graph it is clear that.

  1. 4 students were born in the month of November.
  2. The Maximum number of students were born in the month of August.
Q 2

A family with a monthly income of ₹ 20,000 had planned the following expenditures per month under various heads:

Heads Expenditure
(in thousand rupees)
Grocery 4
Rent 5
Education of children 5
Medicine 2
Fuel 2
Entertainment 1
Miscellaneous 1

Draw a bar graph for the data above.

Solution

We draw the bar graph of this data in the following steps. Note that the unit in the second column is thousand rupees. So, ‘4’ against ‘grocery’ means ₹ 4000.

  1. We represent the Heads (variable) on the horizontal axis choosing any scale, since the width of the bar is not important. But for clarity, we take equal widths for all bars and maintain equal gaps in between. Let one Head be represented by one unit.
  2. We represent the expenditure (value) on the vertical axis. Since the maximum expenditure is ₹ 5000, we can choose the scale as 1 unit = ₹ 1000.
  3. The bar graph is drawn in Fig.
  4. Here, you can easily visualise the relative characteristics of the data at a glance, e.g., the expenditure on education is more than double that of medical expenses. Therefore, in some ways it serves as a better representation of data than the tabular form.
Q 3

A teacher wanted to analyse the performance of two sections of students in a mathematics test of 100 marks. Looking at their performances, she found that a few students got under 20 marks and a few got 70 marks or above. So she decided to group them into intervals of varying sizes as follows: 0 - 20, 20 - 30, . . ., 60 - 70, 70 - 100. Then she formed the following table:

Marks Number of students
0 - 20 7
20 - 30 10
30 - 40 10
40 - 50 20
50 - 60 20
60 - 70 15
70 - above 8
Total 90

Draw a histogram for this table?

Solution

The histrogram is given below.

Q 4

Consider the marks, out of 100, obtained by 51 students of a class in a test, given in Table.

Marks Number of students
0 - 10 5
10 - 20 10
20 - 30 4
30 - 40 6
40 - 50 7
50 - 60 3
60 - 70 2
70 - 80 2
80 - 90 3
90 - 100 9
Total 51

Draw a frequency polygon corresponding to this frequency distribution table.

Solution

The OABCDEFGHIJKL is the frequency polygon, which is shown in Fig.

Frequency polygons can also be drawn independently without drawing histograms. For this, we require the mid-points of the class-intervals used in the data. These mid-points of the class-intervals are called class-marks.

Q 5

In a city, the weekly observations made in a study on the cost of living index are given in the following table:

Cost of living index Number of weeks
140 - 150 5
150 - 160 10
160 - 170 20
170 - 180 9
180 - 190 6
190 - 200 2
Total 52

Draw a frequency polygon for the data above (without constructing a histogram)

Solution

Since we want to draw a frequency polygon without a histogram, let us find the class-marks of the classes given above, that is of 140 - 150, 150 - 160,.... For 140 - 150, the upper limit = 150, and the lower limit = 140
So, the class-mark = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{150+140}{2}=\frac{290}{2}=145 \end{equation}{/tex}
Continuing in the same manner, we find the class-marks of the other classes as well.
So, the new table obtained is as shown in the following table:

Table

Cost of living index Class-marks Number of weeks
140 - 150 145 5
150 - 160 155 10
160 - 170 165 20
170 - 180 175 9
180 - 190 185 6
190 - 200 195 2
Total 52

We can now draw a frequency polygon by plotting the class-marks along the horizontal axis, the frequencies along the vertical-axis, and then plotting and joining the points B(145, 5), C(155, 10), D(165, 20), E(175, 9), F(185, 6) and G(195, 2) by line segments. We should not forget to plot the point corresponding to the class-mark of the class 130 - 140 (just before the lowest class 140 - 150) with zero frequency, that is, A(135, 0), and the point H (205, 0) occurs immediately after G(195, 2). So, the resultant frequency polygon will be ABCDEFGH (see Fig.)

sections.label: Exercise 1.2
sections.html:
Q 1

Let x and y be rational and irrational numbers, respectively. Is x + y necessarily an irrational number? Give an example in support of your answer.

Solution

Here x is rational number and y is irrational number.
Yes, x + y is necessarily an irrational number.
Example: Let x = 2, which is rational.
Let y = {tex}\sqrt2 {/tex}, which is irrational.
Thus x + y = 2 + {tex}\sqrt2 {/tex} = 3.14421356...... which is irrational.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The Sum of a rational and an irrational number is always irrational.

Q 2

Let x be rational and y be irrational. Is xy necessarily irrational? Justify your answer by an example.

Solution

Here x is rational number and y is irrational number.
Yes, xy is necessarily an irrational number.
Example: Let x = 2, which is rational.
Let y = {tex}\sqrt2{/tex}, which is irrational.
Then, x {tex}\times{/tex} y = 2 {tex}\times \sqrt2 = 2\sqrt2{/tex} = 2.82842712474619...........‬.which is again irrational.
Also, consider the case when x = 0.
Then xy = 0, which is rational.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Product of a rational and an irrational number is always irrational, only if the rational number is not zero.

Q 3

Justify your answer: {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}{/tex} is a rational number.

Solution

False
{tex}\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}{/tex} is not a rational number.
Justification: We can write {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}{/tex}  as {tex}\sqrt{2} \times \frac{1}{3}{/tex}.
Here, {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} is irrational and {tex}\frac13{/tex} is rational number.
Product of a rational and an irrational number is always irrational.
{tex}\therefore\frac{\sqrt{2}}{3}{/tex} is irrational.
Hence, it is not rational and thus the given statement is false.

Q 4

There are infinitely many integers between any two integers.

Solution

False.

Justification: Because there are a finite numbers of integers between any two integers. Example: The integers between 1 and 5 are 2, 3 and 4; which are finite in number.

Q 5

Number of rational numbers between 15 and 18 is finite.

Solution

False
Justification: Because there are infinite number of rationals between any two numbers.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The given statement is false.

Q 6

There are numbers which cannot be written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}, q \neq 0, p, q{/tex} both are integers.

Solution

True. Justification: All the irrational numbers are the numbers that cannot be written in the form p/q; q≠0, p, q both are integers and there are infinitely many irrationals.

Q 7

Square of an irrational number is always rational.

Q 8

{tex}\frac{\sqrt{12}}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} is not a rational number as {tex}\sqrt{12}{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} are not integers.

Solution

False
Justification:
{tex}\frac{\sqrt{12}}{\sqrt{3}}=\frac{\sqrt{4} \times \sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}}=\sqrt{4}=2{/tex}
Thus, on simplifying, it turns out to be a rational number.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} The given statement is false.

Q 9

{tex}\frac{\sqrt{15}}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex} is written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}, q \neq 0{/tex} and so it is a rational number.

Solution

False
Justification:
{tex}\frac{\sqrt{15}}{\sqrt{3}}=\frac{\sqrt{5} \times \sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}}=\sqrt{5}{/tex}
Thus, on simplifying, it turns out to be an irrational number again.
{tex}\therefore{/tex}The given statement is false.

Q 10

Classify the number {tex}\sqrt{196}{/tex} as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

{tex}\sqrt{196} {/tex}  is rational number.
Justification:
{tex}\sqrt{196} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{14 \times 14} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{(14)^{2}}{/tex}
=14
Thus, {tex}\sqrt{196}{/tex} is rational.

Q 11

Classify the number {tex}3 \sqrt{18}{/tex} as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

The given number is an irrational number.
Justification:
{tex}3 \sqrt{18}=3 \sqrt{3 \times 3 \times 2}=3 \sqrt{(3)^{2} \times 2}=3 \times 3 \times \sqrt{2}=9 \sqrt{2}{/tex}
Which is product of rational and irrational.Hence irrational.
Thus, 3{tex}\sqrt{18 }{/tex} is irrational.

Q 12

Classify the number {tex}\sqrt{\frac{9}{27}}{/tex} as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

{tex}\sqrt{\frac{9}{27}}=\sqrt{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{1}}{\sqrt{3}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
 {tex}\because{/tex} 1is a rational number and {tex}\sqrt {3}{/tex} ({tex}\ne{/tex} 0) is an irrational number.
{tex}\therefore \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}is an irrational number.
({tex}\because{/tex} The quotient of a non-zero rational number with an irrational number is irrational.)

Hence, {tex} \sqrt{\frac{9}{27}}{/tex}is irrational number. 

Q 13

Classify the number {tex}\frac{\sqrt{28}}{\sqrt{343}}{/tex} as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

{tex}\sqrt{\frac{28}{343}}=\sqrt{\frac{7 \times 4}{49 \times 7}}{/tex} (Simplifying the given expression)
{tex}=\sqrt{\frac{4}{49}}=\frac{2}{7}{/tex}    (Taking the square root)
which is in form {tex}\frac{p}{q}, q \ne 0{/tex}, p, q both are integers.
Thus, {tex}\sqrt{\frac{28}{343}}{/tex} is rational.

Q 14

Classify the number {tex}-\sqrt{0.4}{/tex} as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

The given number is irrational number.
Justification: {tex}-\sqrt{0.4}=-\sqrt{\frac{4}{10}}{/tex} (Simplifying the given expression)
{tex}=-\frac{2}{\sqrt{10}}{/tex}
{tex}=-\frac{2}{\sqrt{10}} \times \frac{\sqrt{10}}{\sqrt{10}}{/tex}
{tex}=-\frac{2 \sqrt{10}}{10}{/tex}
{tex}=-\frac{\sqrt{10}}{5}{/tex}
Which is product of a rational number ({tex}\frac{-1}{5}{/tex}) and an irrational number ({tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex}), and we know that product of rational and irrational results in an irrational number.
Thus, {tex}-\sqrt{0.4}{/tex} is irrational.

Q 15

Classify the number {tex}\frac{\sqrt{12}}{\sqrt{75}}{/tex} as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

The given number is rational number.
Justification: {tex}\sqrt{\frac{12}{75}}=\sqrt{\frac{3 \times 4}{3 \times 25}}{/tex} 
{tex}=\sqrt{\frac{4}{25}}=\frac{2}{5}{/tex} (Taking the square root)
Which is in form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}; q{tex}\ne{/tex}0, p, q both are integers.
Thus, {tex}\sqrt{\frac{12}{75}}{/tex} is rational.

Q 16

Classify the number 0.5918 as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

The given number is rational number.
Justification:
Since 0.5918 is non – repeating terminating decimal number, therefore it can be written in the form p/q; q≠0, where p, q , both are integers.
Therefore, 0.5918 is rational.

Q 17

Classify the number {tex}(1+\sqrt{5})-(4+\sqrt{5}){/tex} as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

The given number is rational number.
Justification:
Consider {tex}(1+\sqrt{5})-(4+\sqrt{5}){/tex} = 1 – 4 + {tex}\sqrt5 - \sqrt5 {/tex}
= 1 – 4
= -3, which is clearly a rational number.
Thus, {tex}(1+\sqrt{5})-(4+\sqrt{5}){/tex} is rational.

Q 18

Classify the number 10.124124... as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

10.124124...  is rational number.
Justification:
Let x = 10.124124...  is non - terminating recurring decimal number, therefore it can be written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}, q \ne 0{/tex}, p, q both are integers.
Thus, 10.124124 is rational.

Q 19

Classify the number 1.010010001... as rational or irrational with justification.

Solution

The given number is irrational number.
Justification: Since 1.010010001...................... is non - terminating non - recurring decimal number, therefore it cannot be written in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q }, q \ne 0{/tex}, p, q both are integers.
Thus, 1.010010001 ......is irrational.

sections.label: Exercise 1.3
sections.html:
Q 1

Find which of the variables x, y, z and u represent rational numbers and which irrational numbers :

  1. x2 = 5
  2. y2 = 9
  3. z2 = .04
  4. {tex}u^{2}=\frac{17}{4}{/tex}
Solution
  1. x2 = 5
    On taking square root on both sides, we get
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = ± {tex}\sqrt{5}{/tex} 
    Since we know that {tex}\sqrt{p}{/tex} is always irrational number where p is prime number
    So, x is an irrational number.
  2. y2 = 9
    On taking square root on both sides, we get
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = ± 3
    So, y is a rational number
  3. z2 = .04
    On taking square root on both sides, we get
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = ± 0.2
    So, z is a rational number.
  4. {tex}u^{2}=\frac{17}{4}{/tex}
    On taking square root on both sides, we get
    {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{u}=\pm \frac{\sqrt{17}}{2}{/tex}
    So, u is an irrational number because {tex}\sqrt{17}{/tex} is irrational.
Q 2

Find three rational numbers between -1 and -2.

Solution

-1.1, -1.2, -1.3 (terminating decimals) are three rational numbers lying between -1 and -2.

Q 3

Find three rational numbers between 0.1 and 0.11

Solution

Q 4

Find three rational numbers between {tex}\frac{5}{7}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{6}{7}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}\frac{5}{7} = \frac{5}{7} \times \frac{{10}}{{10}} = \frac{{50}}{{70}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{6}{7} = \frac{6}{7} \times \frac{{10}}{{10}} = \frac{{60}}{{70}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{51}}{{70}},\frac{{52}}{{70}},\frac{{53}}{{70}}{/tex} are three rational numbers lying and between {tex}\frac{{50}}{{70}}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{{60}}{{70}}{/tex} and therefore lie between {tex}\frac{5}{7}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{6}{7}{/tex}.

Now, {tex}\sqrt 2 \times \sqrt 3 \frac{{18}}{{80}}\left( { = \frac{9}{{40}}} \right),\frac{{19}}{{80}}{/tex} are three rational numbers lying between and {tex}\frac{1}{4}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}.

Q 6

Insert a rational and irrational number between 2 and 3.

Solution

The given numbers are 2 and 3
Now we know that:
If a and b are two positive rational numbers such that ab is not a perfect square of a rational number, then {tex}\sqrt{ab}{/tex} is an irrational number lying between a and b.
Also, if a, b are rational numbers, then {tex}\frac{a+b}{2}{/tex} is a rational number between them.
Here a = 2 and b = 3
then ab = 2{tex}\times{/tex}3 = 6 which is not a perfect square
So, an irrational number between 2 and 3 = {tex}\sqrt{2 \times 3}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A rational number between 2 and 3 is {tex}\frac{2+3}{2}{/tex} = 2.5

{tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1 \times 2}{3 \times 2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1 \times 3}{2 \times 3}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{3}{6}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{2}{6}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{2\times 2}{6 \times 2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{4}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3}{6}{/tex}= {tex}\frac{3 \times 2}{6 \times 2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{6}{12}{/tex}
Rational number between {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} is {tex}\frac{5}{12}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{3}=0.33 {/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{2}=0.5 {/tex}
Irrational number between {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} and {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} is 0.4040040004....

  1. 7

Q 17

Locate {tex}\sqrt{5}, \sqrt{10}{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt{17}{/tex} on the number line.

Solution
  1. {tex}\sqrt5{/tex}
    To represent{tex}\sqrt {5}{/tex} on number line , we consider Pythagoras Theorem 
    i.e. H2 = P2 + B2  
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt {5}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{1^2+2^2}{/tex}
    On the number line, take OA = 2 units.

    Draw BA = 1 units, perpendicular to OA. Join OB.

    Using a compass with centre O and radius OB, draw an arc which intersects the number line at the point C. Then, C corresponds to {tex}\sqrt5{/tex}
  2. {tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex}
    To represent{tex}\sqrt {10}{/tex} on number line , we consider Pythagoras Theorem 
    i.e. H2 = P2 + B2  
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{1^2+3^2}{/tex}
    On the number line, take OA = 3 units.

    Draw BA = 1 units, perpendicular to OA. Join OB.

    Using a compass with centre O and radius OB, draw an arc which intersects the number line at the point C. Then, C corresponds to {tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex}.
  3. {tex}\sqrt{17}{/tex}
    To represent{tex}\sqrt {5}{/tex} on number line , we consider Pythagoras Theorem 
    i.e. H2 = P2 + B2  
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\sqrt{17}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{1^2+4^2}{/tex}
    On the number line, take OA = 4 units.

    Draw BA = 1 units, perpendicular to OA. Join OB.

    Using a compass with centre O and radius OB, draw an arc which intersects the number line at the point C. Then, C corresponds to {tex}\sqrt{17}{/tex}.
Q 18

Represent {tex}\sqrt{4.5}{/tex} on the number line.

Solution

Consider, AB = 4.5 units. 
Extend AB upto point C such that BC = 1 unit.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AC = 4.5 + 1 = 5.5 units. 
Now mark O as the midpoint of AC.
With O as centre and radius OC draw a semicircle.
Draw perpendicular BD on AC which intersect the semicircle at D.

This length BD = {tex}\sqrt{4.5}{/tex} units.
To show BD on the number line, consider line ABC as number line with point B as zero.
Therefore, {tex}BC = 1 \ unit.{/tex}
With B as centre and radius BD draw an arc which intersects number line ABC at E.
So, point E represents {tex}\sqrt{4.5}{/tex}
AB = 4.5 units
BC = 1 unit
{tex}BD = BE ={/tex} {tex}\sqrt{4.5} \ units{/tex}

Q 19

Represent {tex}\sqrt{5.6}{/tex} on the number line.

Solution

Let us draw a line segment AB = 5.6 units and extend it to C such that BC = 1 unit. Let O be the mid-point of AC.
Now let us draw a semi-circle with center O and radius OA. Now, draw BD perpendicular to AC passing through point B and intersecting the semi-circle at point D.
Hence, the distance BD is {tex}\sqrt{5.6}{/tex} units.
Draw an arc with center B and radius BD, meeting AC produced at E, then BE = BD = {tex}\sqrt{5.6}{/tex} units.

Q 20

Represent {tex}\sqrt{8.1}{/tex} on the number line.

Solution

Let us draw a line segment AB = 8.1 units and extend it to point C such that BC = 1 unit. Let O be the mid-point of AC. Let us draw a semi-circle with center 0 and radius OA
Now, draw BD perpendicular to AC passing through point B intersecting the semi-circle at point D.
Hence, the distance BD is {tex}\sqrt{8.1}{/tex} units.
Draw an arc with center B and radius BD, meeting AC produced at E, then BE = BD = {tex}\sqrt{8.1}{/tex} units.

Q 21

Represent {tex}\sqrt{2.3}{/tex} on the number line.

Solution

Let us draw a line segment AB = 2.3 units and extend it to C such that BC = 1 unit. Let O be the mid-point of AC.
Now let us draw a semi-circle with center O and radius OA.
Now, draw BD perpendicular to AC passing through point B and intersecting the semi-circle at point D.

Hence, the distance BD is {tex}\sqrt {2.3}{/tex} units.
Draw an arc with centre B and radius BD, meeting AC produced at E, then BE = BD = {tex}\sqrt {2.3}{/tex} units.

Q 23

Express 0.888... in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q \ne 0{/tex}.

Solution

Let x = 0.888... = {tex}0.\overline 8{/tex} …(1)
{tex}\therefore \;10x = 8.\overline 8{/tex} …(2)
Subtracting (1) and (2), we get
9x = 8
Hence, {tex}x = \frac{8}{9}{/tex}

Q 24

Express {tex}5.\overline 2{/tex} in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q \ne 0{/tex}.

Solution

Let x = {tex}5.\overline 2{/tex} = 5.2222...  …(1)
Multiplying both sides by 10, we get
10x = 52.222... = {tex}52.\overline 2{/tex} …(2)
If we subtract {tex}5.\overline 2{/tex} from {tex}52.\overline 2{/tex}, the repeating portion the decimal cancels out.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Subtracting (1) and (2), we get
10x - x = 47 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9x = 47 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{{47}}{9}{/tex}
Hence, {tex}5.\overline 2 = \frac{{47}}{9}{/tex}.

Q 25

Express {tex}0. \overline { 001 }{/tex} in the form {tex}\frac { p } { q }{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q \neq 0 {/tex}

Solution

Let x = {tex}0. \overline { 001 }{/tex} = 0.001001001 . . . .
Multiplying both sides by 1000 (since three digits are repeating), we get
1000x = 1.001001 . . . .
⇒ 1000x =  1+ 0.001001001 . . . .
⇒ 1000x = 1 + x
⇒ 1000x – x = 1
⇒ 999x = 1
⇒ {tex}x = \frac { 1 } { 999 }{/tex}
Thus {tex}0 . \overline { 001 } = \frac { 1 } { 999 }{/tex}
Here  p = 1
q = {tex}999\left( { \ne 0} \right){/tex}

Q 26

Express 0.2555.... in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q \ne 0{/tex}.

90x = 23

Q 27

Express {tex}0.1\overline {34}{/tex} in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q \ne 0{/tex}.

Multiplying both sides by 100, we get
100x = 13.43434... = {tex}13.4\overline {34}{/tex} …(2)
If we subtract {tex}0.1\overline {34}{/tex} from {tex}13.4\overline {34}{/tex}, the represent portion of the decimal cancels out.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} subtracting (1) from (2), we get
100x - x = 13.3 {tex}\Rightarrow 99x = \frac{{133}}{{10}} \Rightarrow x = \frac{{133}}{{990}}{/tex}
Hence, {tex}0.1\overline {34} = \frac{{133}}{{990}}{/tex}.

Express .00323232… in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q \ne 0{/tex}.

Q 30

Show that 0.142857142857 ...  {tex}=\frac{1}{7}{/tex}

Solution

Let x =  {tex}0.\overline {142857} {/tex} ...(i)
Then {tex}10,00,000x = 142857{/tex}{tex}.\overline {142857} {/tex} ...(ii)

Q 32

Simplify : {tex}\frac{{\sqrt {24} }}{8} + \frac{{\sqrt {54} }}{9}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{\sqrt {24} }}{8} + \frac{{\sqrt {54} }}{9} = \frac{{\sqrt {4 \times 6} }}{8} + \frac{{\sqrt {9 \times 6} }}{9}{/tex} {tex}= \frac{{2\sqrt 6 }}{8} + \frac{{3\sqrt 6 }}{9} = \frac{{\sqrt 6 }}{4} + \frac{{\sqrt 6 }}{3}{/tex}
{tex}= \sqrt 6 \left( {\frac{1}{4} + \frac{1}{3}} \right) = \sqrt 6 \left( {\frac{{3 + 4}}{{12}}} \right) = \frac{{7\sqrt 6 }}{{12}}{/tex}

Q 33

Simplify: {tex}\sqrt[4]{12} \times \sqrt[7]{6}.{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\sqrt[4]{12} \times \sqrt[7]{6}=(2 \times 2 \times 3)^{1 / 4} \times(2 \times 3)^{1 / 7} \qquad\left[\because \sqrt[n]{a}=a^{1 / n}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=2^{1 / 4} \times 2^{1 / 4} \times 3^{1 / 4} \times 2^{1 / 7} \times 3^{1 / 7}{/tex}
{tex}=2^{1 / 4+1 / 4+1 / 7} \times 3^{1 / 4+1 / 7}=2^{1 / 2+1 / 7} \times 3^{1 / 4+1 / 7}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because a^{m} \times a^{n}=a^{m+n}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=2^{9 / 14} \times 3^{11 / 28}=\sqrt[14]{2^{9}} \times \sqrt[28]{3^{11}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt[28]{2^{18}} \times \sqrt[28]{3^{11}}=\sqrt[28]{2^{18} \times 3^{11}}[\because \sqrt[7]{a} \times \sqrt[7]{b}=\sqrt[n]{a b}]{/tex}

{tex}4 \sqrt{28}+3 \sqrt{7}+\sqrt[3]{7}{/tex}
 ={tex}4 \sqrt{4 \times 7}+3 \sqrt{7}+{7}^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}{tex}\left[\because \sqrt[n]{a}=a^{1 / n}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}{8 \sqrt{7}}+{3 \sqrt{7}}+7^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}
={tex}11\sqrt{7} +7^{\frac{1}{3}}{/tex}

Q 35

Simplify: {tex}3\sqrt 3 + 2\sqrt {27} + \frac{7}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}3\sqrt 3 + 2\sqrt {27} + \frac{7}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}
{tex} = 3\sqrt 3 + 2 \times 3\sqrt 3 + \frac{7}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex} {tex}= 3\sqrt 3 + 6\sqrt 3 + \frac{7}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

{tex} = \sqrt 3 \left( {3 + 6 + \frac{7}{3}} \right){/tex} {tex}= \sqrt 3 \left( {9 + \frac{7}{3}} \right) = \sqrt 3 \times \frac{{34}}{3}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{34}}{3}\sqrt 3{/tex}

Q 36

Q 37

Simplify: {tex}\sqrt[4]{81}-8 \sqrt[3]{216}+15 \sqrt[5]{32}+\sqrt{225}.{/tex}

Solution

Given,  {tex}\sqrt[4]{81}-8 \sqrt[3]{216}+15 \sqrt[5]{32}+\sqrt{225}{/tex}
{tex}=\left(3^{4}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}}-8\left(6^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}+15\left(2^{5}\right)^{\frac{1}{5}}+\left(15^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex}
{tex}= 3 - 8 \times 6 + 15 \times 2 + 15 \\= 3 - 48 + 30 + 15 \\= 48 - 48\\ = 0{/tex}
 

Q 38

Simplify: {tex}\frac{3}{{\sqrt 8 }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{3}{{\sqrt 8 }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{3}{{\sqrt {4 \times 2} }} + \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }} = \frac{1}{{\sqrt 2 }}\left( {\frac{3}{2} + 1} \right){/tex}
{tex}= \frac{5}{{2\sqrt 2 }} = \frac{5}{{2\sqrt 2 }} \times \frac{{\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 2 }} = \frac{{5\sqrt 2 }}{4}{/tex}
 

Q 39

Q 40

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{2}{{3\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

{tex}\frac{2}{{3\sqrt 3 }}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{2}{{3\sqrt 3 }} \times \frac{{\sqrt 3 }}{{\sqrt 3 }} = \frac{{2\sqrt 3 }}{9}{/tex}

Q 41

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{\sqrt {40} }}{{\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

Solution

Q 42

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}{tex}= \frac{{3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 2 }} \times \frac{{\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 2 }} = \frac{{\sqrt 2 (3 + \sqrt 2 )}}{{4 \times 2}} = \frac{{3\sqrt 2 + 2}}{8}{/tex}

Q 43

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{16}}{{\sqrt {41} - 5}}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{16}}{{\sqrt {41} - 5}} = \frac{{16}}{{\sqrt {41} - 5}} \times \frac{{\sqrt {41} + 5}}{{\sqrt {41} + 5}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{16(\sqrt {41} + 5)}}{{41 - 25}} = \frac{{16(\sqrt {41} + 5)}}{{16}}{/tex}
{tex}= \sqrt {41} + 5{/tex}

Q 44

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{2 + \sqrt 3 }}{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{2 + \sqrt 3 }}{{2 - \sqrt 3 }} = \frac{{2 + \sqrt 3 }}{{2 - \sqrt 3 }} \times \frac{{2 + \sqrt 3 }}{{2 + \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{{{(2 + \sqrt 3 )}^2}}}{{{{(2)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}} = \frac{{4 + 3 + 4\sqrt 3 }}{{4 - 3}}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{7 + 4\sqrt 3 }}{1} = 7 + 4\sqrt 3{/tex}

Q 45

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{\sqrt 6 }}{{\sqrt 2 + \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{\sqrt 6 }}{{\sqrt 2 + \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}{tex}= \frac{{\sqrt 6 }}{{\sqrt 2 + \sqrt 3 }} \times \frac{{\sqrt 2 - \sqrt 3 }}{{\sqrt 2 - \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{\sqrt 6 (\sqrt 2 - \sqrt 3 )}}{{{{(\sqrt 2 )}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}} = \frac{{\sqrt 6 (\sqrt 2 - \sqrt 3 )}}{{2 - 3}}{/tex}
{tex}= \sqrt 6 (\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 ) = \sqrt {6 \times 3} - \sqrt {6 \times 2}{/tex}
{tex} = \sqrt {18} - \sqrt {12} = \sqrt {9 \times 2} - \sqrt {4 \times 3}{/tex}{tex}= 3\sqrt 2 - 2\sqrt 3{/tex}
 

Q 46

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 }} = \frac{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 }} \times \frac{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{{{(\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 )}^2}}}{{{{(\sqrt 3 )}^2} - {{(\sqrt 2 )}^2}}} = \frac{{3 + 2 + 2\sqrt 3 \times \sqrt 2 }}{{3 - 2}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{5 + 2\sqrt 6 }}{1} = 5 + 2\sqrt 6{/tex}

Q 47

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{3\sqrt 5 + \sqrt 3 }}{{\sqrt 5 - \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{3\sqrt 5 + \sqrt 3 }}{{\sqrt 5 - \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}{tex}= \frac{{3\sqrt 5 + \sqrt 3 }}{{\sqrt 5 - \sqrt 3 }} \times \frac{{\sqrt 5 + \sqrt 3 }}{{\sqrt 5 + \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{15 + 3\sqrt {15} + \sqrt {15} + 3}}{{{{(\sqrt {15} )}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{18 + 4\sqrt {15} }}{{5 - 3}} = \frac{{2(9 + 2\sqrt {15} )}}{2}{/tex}
{tex} = 9 + 2\sqrt {15}{/tex}

Q 48

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{{4\sqrt 3 + 5\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt {48} + \sqrt {18} }}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{4\sqrt 3 + 5\sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt {48} + \sqrt {18} }} = \frac{{4\sqrt 3 + 5\sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 3 + 3\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{4\sqrt 3 + 5\sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 3 + 3\sqrt 2 }} \times \frac{{4\sqrt 3 - 3\sqrt 2 }}{{4\sqrt 3 - 3\sqrt 2 }}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{48 - 12\sqrt 6 + 20\sqrt 6 - 30}}{{{{(4\sqrt 3 )}^2} - {{(3\sqrt 2 )}^2}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{18 + 8\sqrt 6 }}{{48 - 18}} = \frac{{18 + 8\sqrt 6 }}{{30}} = \frac{{9 + 4\sqrt 6 }}{{15}}{/tex}

Q 49

Find the value of a and b: {tex}\frac { 5 + 2 \sqrt { 3 } } { 7 + 4 \sqrt { 3 } } = a - 6 \sqrt { 3 }{/tex}

Solution

LHS {tex}= \frac { 5 + 2 \sqrt { 3 } } { 7 + 4 \sqrt { 3 } } = \frac { 5 + 2 \sqrt { 3 } } { 7 + 4 \sqrt { 3 } } \times \frac { 7 - 4 \sqrt { 3 } } { 7 - 4 \sqrt { 3 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { ( 5 + 2 \sqrt { 3 } ) ( 7 - 4 \sqrt { 3 } ) } { ( 7 ) ^ { 2 } - ( 4 \sqrt { 3 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 35 - 20 \sqrt { 3 } + 14 \sqrt { 3 } - 24 } { 49 - 48 }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 11 - 6 \sqrt { 3 } } { 1 } = 11 - 6 \sqrt { 3 }{/tex}

Now, {tex}11 - 6 \sqrt { 3 } = a - 6 \sqrt { 3 }{/tex} [comparing LHS and RHS]

By comparing on both sides,

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 11

Q 50

Find the value of a and b: {tex}\frac { 3 - \sqrt { 5 } } { 3 + 2 \sqrt { 5 } } = a \sqrt { 5 } - \frac { 19 } { 11 }{/tex}

Solution

LHS {tex}= \frac { 3 - \sqrt { 5 } } { 3 + 2 \sqrt { 5 } } = \frac { 3 - \sqrt { 5 } } { 3 + 2 \sqrt { 5 } } \times \frac { 3 - 2 \sqrt { 5 } } { 3 - 2 \sqrt { 5 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { ( 3 - \sqrt { 5 } ) ( 3 - 2 \sqrt { 5 } ) } { ( 3 ) ^ { 2 }- ( 2 \sqrt { 5 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 9 - 6 \sqrt { 5 } - 3 \sqrt { 5 } + 10 } { 9 - 20 } = \frac { 19 - 9 \sqrt { 5 } } { - 11 }{/tex}

Now, {tex}\frac { 19 - 9 \sqrt { 5 } } { - 11 } = a \sqrt { 5 } - \frac { 19 } { 11 }{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \frac { 9 } { 11 } \sqrt { 5 } - \frac { 19 } { 11 } = a \sqrt { 5 } - \frac { 19 } { 11 }{/tex}

By comparing on both sides, we get

{tex}a = \frac { 9 } { 11 }{/tex}

Q 51

Find the value of a and b: {tex}\frac { \sqrt { 2 } + \sqrt { 3 } } { 3 \sqrt { 2 } - 2 \sqrt { 3 } } = 2 - b \sqrt { 6 }{/tex}

Solution

LHS {tex}= \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + \sqrt { 3 } } { 3 \sqrt { 2 } - 2 \sqrt { 3 } } = \frac { \sqrt { 2 } + \sqrt { 3 } } { 3 \sqrt { 2 } - 2 \sqrt { 3 } } \times \frac { 3 \sqrt { 2 } + 2 \sqrt { 3 } } { 3 \sqrt { 2 } + 2 \sqrt { 3 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { ( \sqrt { 2 } + \sqrt { 3 } ) ( 3 \sqrt { 2 } + 2 \sqrt { 3 } ) } { ( 3 \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } - ( 2 \sqrt { 3 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 6 + 2 \sqrt { 6 } + 3 \sqrt { 6 } + 6 } { 18 - 12 }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 12 + 5 \sqrt { 6 } } { 6 } \\= 2 + \frac { 5 \sqrt { 6 } } { 6 }{/tex}

Now, {tex}2 - b \sqrt { 6 } = 2 + \frac { 5 } { 6 } \sqrt { 6 }{/tex}

By comparing on both sides, we get

{tex}\Rightarrow b = - \frac { 5 } { 6 }{/tex}

Q 52

Find the values of a and b {tex}\frac{{7 + \sqrt 5 }}{{7 - \sqrt 5 }} - \frac{{7 - \sqrt 5 }}{{7 + \sqrt 5 }} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{{7 + \sqrt 5 }}{{7 - \sqrt 5 }} - \frac{{7 - \sqrt 5 }}{{7 + \sqrt 5 }} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{7 + \sqrt 5 }}{{7 - \sqrt 5 }} \times \frac{{7 + \sqrt 5 }}{{7 + \sqrt 5 }} - \frac{{7 - \sqrt 5 }}{{7 + \sqrt 5 }} \times \frac{{7 - \sqrt 5 }}{{7 - \sqrt 5 }}{/tex}{tex} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{{{(7 + \sqrt 5 )}^2}}}{{{{(7)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 5 )}^2}}} - \frac{{{{(7 - \sqrt 5 )}^2}}}{{{{(7)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 5 )}^2}}}{/tex}{tex} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}
{tex}\frac{{49 + 5 + 14\sqrt 5 }}{{49 - 5}} - \frac{{49 + 5 - 14\sqrt 5 }}{{49 - 5}}{/tex}{tex} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{54 + 14\sqrt 5 }}{{44}} - \frac{{54 - 14\sqrt 5 }}{{44}} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{54 + 14\sqrt 5 - 54 + 14\sqrt 5 }}{{44}}{/tex}
{tex} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b = \frac{{28\sqrt 5 }}{{44}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{{7\sqrt 5 }}{{11}} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow 0 + \frac{{7\sqrt 5 }}{{11}} = a + \frac{7}{{11}}\sqrt 5 b{/tex}
Thus, a = 0 and b = 1.

Q 53

If {tex}a = 2 + \sqrt 3{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}a - \frac{1}{a}{/tex}.

Solution

We have {tex}a = 2 + \sqrt 3{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \;\frac{1}{a} = \frac{1}{{2 + \sqrt 3 }} = \frac{1}{{2 + \sqrt 3 }} \times \frac{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}{/tex}{tex} = \frac{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}{{{{(2)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}{{4 - 3}} = \frac{{2 - \sqrt 3 }}{1} = 2 - \sqrt 3{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \;a - \frac{1}{a} = 2 + \sqrt 3 - 2 + \sqrt 3 = 2\sqrt 3{/tex}
 

Q 54

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac 4{\sqrt3}{/tex} and hence evaluate by taking {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = 1.414, {tex}\sqrt3{/tex} =1.732 and {tex}\sqrt5{/tex} = 2.236 up to three places of decimal.

Solution

Let {tex}E=\frac{4}{\sqrt{3}}{/tex}
For rationalising the denominator, multiplying numerator and denominator by {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}, we get
{tex}E =\frac{4}{\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{3}} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{4 \sqrt{3}}{3} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{4}{3} \times 1.732 {/tex}  [put {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} = 1.732]
={tex} \frac{6.928}{3} {/tex}
=​​​​​​​ 2.309 

Q 55

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac 6{\sqrt6}{/tex} and hence evaluate by taking {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = 1.414, {tex}\sqrt3{/tex}=1.732 and {tex}\sqrt5{/tex} = 2.236 up to three places of decimal.

Solution

Let E = {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{6}}{/tex}
For rationalising the denominator, multiplying numerator and denominator by {tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}, we get
E = {tex}\frac{6}{\sqrt{6}} \times \frac{\sqrt{6}}{\sqrt{6}} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6 \sqrt{6}}{6} {/tex}
={tex}\sqrt{2} \times \sqrt{3} {/tex}  [ put {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} = 1.414 and {tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex} = 1.732 ]
= 1.414 {tex}\times{/tex} 1.732
= 2.449  

Q 56

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac {\sqrt{10} - \sqrt5}2{/tex} and hence evaluate by taking {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = 1.414, {tex}\sqrt3{/tex}=1.732 and {tex}\sqrt5{/tex} = 2.236 up to three places of decimal.

Solution

Let E = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{10}-\sqrt{5}}{2} {/tex}
= {tex} \frac{\sqrt{5} \sqrt{2}-\sqrt{5}}{2} {/tex}
 = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{5}(\sqrt{2}-1)}{2} \quad[\because \sqrt{10}=\sqrt{2} \cdot \sqrt{5}]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2.236(1.414-1)}{2} {/tex}
= {tex}1.118 \times 0.414 {/tex} 
= 0.46286 
= 0.4628

Q 57

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac {\sqrt{2}} {2+\sqrt2}{/tex} and hence evaluate by taking {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = 1.414, {tex}\sqrt3{/tex} =1.732 and {tex}\sqrt5{/tex} = 2.236 up to three places of decimal.

Solution

 {tex}\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2+\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{2-\sqrt{2}}{2-\sqrt{2}}{/tex}    (Rationalise the denominator)

{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{2}(2-\sqrt{2})}{(2)^{2}-(\sqrt{2})^{2}}{/tex}         [using identity, (a - b)(a + b) = a2 - b2]
{tex}=\frac{2\sqrt2-2}{4-2}=\frac{2(\sqrt{2}-1)}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} - 1 = 1.414 - 1 [using  {tex}\sqrt2{/tex} = 1.414]

= 0.414

Q 58

Rationalise the denominator of {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex} and hence evaluate by taking {tex}\sqrt{2}=1.414{/tex}, {tex}\sqrt{3}=1.732{/tex} and {tex}\sqrt5 = 2.236{/tex}, up to three places of decimal.

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}+\sqrt{2}} \times \frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{(\sqrt{3})^{2}-(\sqrt{2})^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}}{3-2}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{3}-\sqrt{2}{/tex}
= 1.732 - 1.414
= 0.318

Q 59

Simplify: {tex}\left(1^{3}+2^{3}+3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\left(1^{3}+2^{3}+3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}(1+8+27)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}(36)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}\left(6^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}} {/tex}
= {tex}6^{2 \times \frac{1}{2}}{/tex} {tex}\left[\because\left(a^{m}\right)^{n}=a^{m n}\right]{/tex}
=6
 

Q 60

Simplify: {tex}\frac{3}{5}^{4} \frac{8}{5}^{-12} \frac{32}{5}^6{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\left(\frac{3}{5}\right)^{4}\left(\frac{8}{5}\right)^{-12}\left(\frac{32}{5}\right)^{6}{/tex} {tex}=\frac{3^{4}}{5^{4}} \times\left(\frac{5}{2^{3}}\right)^{12} \times\left(\frac{2^{5}}{5}\right)^{6}\left(\because a^{-1}=\frac{1}{a}\right){/tex}
{tex}=\frac{3^{4}}{5^{4}} \times \frac{5^{12}}{2^{36}} \times \frac{2^{30}}{5^{6}}{/tex}   [ using {tex}\left(a^{m}\right)^{n}=a^{m n}{/tex} ]
{tex}=\frac{3^{4} \times 5^{12-4-6}}{2^{36-30}}{/tex} [ using  {tex} \frac{a^{m}}{a^{n}}=a^{m-n}{/tex}]

{tex}=\frac{3^{4}}{2^{6}} \times 5^{2}=\frac{81 \times 25}{64}=\frac{2025}{64}{/tex}

Q 61

Simplify: {tex}\left(\frac{1}{27}\right)^{\frac{-2}{3}}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\left(\frac{1}{27}\right)^{-2 / 3}=\left(\frac{1}{3^{3}}\right)^{-2 / 3}{/tex}

= {tex}\frac1{3^{3\times\left({\frac{-2}3}\right)}}{/tex}          {tex}\left[\because\left(a^{m}\right)^{n}=a^{m n}\right]{/tex}

= {tex}\frac1{3^{-2}}{/tex}

= 32          {tex}\left[\because \frac{1}{a}=a^{-1}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=9{/tex}

Q 62

Simplify: {tex}\left.\left[((625)^{-\frac{1}{2}}\right)^{-\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{2}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\left.\left[ ( (625)^{-\frac{1}{2}}\right)^{-\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{2}{/tex} = {tex}\left.\left[((5^4)^{-\frac{1}{2}}\right)^{-\frac{1}{4}}\right]^{2}{/tex}

= {tex}5^{4\times\left(\frac{-1}2\right)\times\left(\frac{-1}4\right)\times2}{/tex} [ using (am)n = amn]

= 51 = 5

Q 63

Simplify: {tex}\frac{9^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 27^{-\frac{1}{2}}}{3^{\frac{1}{6}} \times 3^{-\frac{2}{3}}}.{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{9^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 27^{-\frac{1}{2}}}{3^{\frac{1}{6}} \times 3^{-\frac{2}{3}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{9^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 3^{\frac{2}{3}}}{3^{\frac{1}{6}} \times 27^{\frac{1}{2}}}\\=\frac{\left(3^{2}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 3^{\frac{2}{3}}}{3^{\frac{1}{6}} \times\left(3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{2}}}\\=\frac{3^{\frac{2}{3}} \times 3^{\frac{2}{3}}}{3^{\frac{1}{6}} \times 3^{\frac{3}{2}}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{3^{\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2}{3}}}{3^{\frac{1}{6}+\frac{3}{2}}}\\=\frac{3^{\frac{4}{3}}}{3^{\frac{1+9}{6}}}\\=\frac{3^{\frac{4}{3}}}{3^{\frac{10}{6}}}\\=3^{\frac{4}{3}-\frac{5}{3}}\\=3^{-\frac{1}{3}}\\=\frac{1}{3^{\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex}

Q 64

Simplify: {tex}64^{-\frac{1}{3}}\left[64^{\frac{1}{3}}-64^{\frac{2}{3}}\right]{/tex}

Solution

{tex}64^{-\frac{1}{3}}\left[64^{\frac{1}{3}}-64^{\frac{2}{3}}\right]{/tex}

{tex}=\left(4^{3}\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}\left[\left(4^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}-\left(4^{3}\right)^{\frac{2}{3}}\right]{/tex}

= {tex}4^\frac{-3}3{/tex}{tex}\left(4^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}}-4^{3 \times \frac{2}{3}}\right)=4^{-1}\left(4-4^{2}\right){/tex}{tex}\left[\because\left(a^{m}\right)^{n}=a^{m n}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{4}(4-16)=-\frac{12}{4}=-3{/tex}   [ using {tex}a^{-1}=\;\frac1a]{/tex}

Q 65

Simplify: {tex}\frac{8^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 16^{\frac{1}{3}}}{32^{-\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\frac{8^{\frac{1}{3}} \times 16^{\frac{1}{3}}}{32^{-\frac{1}{3}}}=\frac{\left(2^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}} \times\left(2^{4}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}}{\left(2^{5}\right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}}{/tex}

{tex}=\frac{2^{3 \times \frac{1}{3}} \times 2^{4 \times \frac{1}{3}}}{2^{5 \times(-\frac{1}{3})}}{/tex}  [ using {tex}\left(a^{m}\right)^{n}=a^{m n}{/tex}]
{tex}=\frac{2^{\frac{3}{3}+\frac{4}{3}}}{2^{-\frac{5}{3}}}=\frac{2^{\frac{7}{3}}}{2^{-\frac{5}{3}}}=2^{\frac{7}{3}+\frac{5}{3}}=2^{\frac{12}{3}}{/tex} = 24 = 16 {tex}\left[\because \frac{a^{m}}{a^{n}}=a^{m-n}\right]{/tex}

sections.html:
Q 1

Express {tex}0.6 + 0.\overline 7 + 0.4\overline {7}{/tex} in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and {tex}q \ne 0{/tex}.

Solution

We have {tex}0.6 = \frac{6}{{10}}{/tex} 
Let {tex}x = 0.\bar 7 = 0.777...{/tex} (1)
Subtracting (1) from (2), we get
9x = 7 {tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{7}{9}{/tex} or {tex}0.\bar 7 = \frac{7}{9}{/tex}
Now, let {tex}y = 0.4\bar 7 = 0.4777...{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \;10y = 4.\bar 7{/tex} ...(3)
And {tex}100y = 47.\bar 7{/tex} ...(4)
Subtracting (3) from (4), we get
90y = 43 {tex}\Rightarrow y = \frac{{43}}{{90}}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \;0.4\bar 7 = \frac{{43}}{{90}}{/tex}
Now, {tex}0.6 + 0.\bar 7 + 0.4\bar 7{/tex}{tex} = \frac{6}{{10}} + \frac{7}{9} + \frac{{43}}{{90}}{/tex}{tex}= \frac{{54 + 70 + 43}}{{90}} = \frac{{167}}{{90}}{/tex}
So, {tex}\frac{{167}}{{90}}{/tex} is of the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex} and {tex}q \ne 0{/tex}.

Q 2

Simplify: {tex}\frac{7 \sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{10}+\sqrt{3}}-\frac{2 \sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{5}}-\frac{3 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{15}+3 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}.

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac{7 \sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{10}+\sqrt{3}}-\frac{2 \sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{5}}-\frac{3 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{15}+3 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{7 \sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{10}+\sqrt{3}} \times \frac{\sqrt{10}-\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{10}-\sqrt{3}}-\frac{2 \sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{6}+\sqrt{5}} \times \frac{\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{5}}{\sqrt{6}-\sqrt{5}}-\frac{3 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{15}+3 \sqrt{2}} \times \frac{\sqrt{15}-3 \sqrt{2}}{\sqrt{15}-3 \sqrt{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{7 \sqrt{3}(\sqrt{10}-\sqrt{3})}{(\sqrt{10})^{2}-(\sqrt{3})^{2}}-\frac{2 \sqrt{30}-2 \times 5}{(\sqrt{6})^{2}-(\sqrt{5})^{2}}-\frac{3 \sqrt{30}-18}{(\sqrt{15})^2-(3\sqrt2)^2}{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{7(\sqrt{30}-3)}{10-3}-\frac{(2 \sqrt{30}-10)}{6-5}-\frac{3 \sqrt{30}-18}{15-18}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{30}-3-(2 \sqrt{30}-10)-(6-\sqrt{30}){/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{30}-3-2 \sqrt{30}+10-6+\sqrt{30}{/tex}
{tex}=10-9+2 \sqrt{30}-2 \sqrt{30}=1{/tex}

Q 3

If {tex}\sqrt { 2 } = 1.414 , \sqrt { 3 } = 1.732{/tex} then find the value of {tex}\frac { 4 } { 3 \sqrt { 3 } - 2 \sqrt { 2 } } + \frac { 3 } { 3 \sqrt { 3 } + 2 \sqrt { 2 } }{/tex}

Solution

Given, {tex}\frac { 4 } { 3 \sqrt { 3 } - 2 \sqrt { 2 } } + \frac { 3 } { 3 \sqrt { 3 } + 2 \sqrt { 2 } }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 4(3 \sqrt { 3 } + 2 \sqrt { 2 } )+ 3 ( 3 \sqrt { 3 } - 2 \sqrt { 2 } ) } { ( 3 \sqrt { 3 } - 2 \sqrt { 2 } ) ( 3 \sqrt { 3 } + 2 \sqrt { 2 } ) }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 12 \sqrt { 3 } + 8 \sqrt { 2 } + 9 \sqrt { 3 } - 6 \sqrt { 2 } } { ( 3 \sqrt { 3 } ) ^ { 2 } - ( 2 \sqrt { 2 } ) ^ { 2 } }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 21 \sqrt { 3 } + 2 \sqrt { 2 } } { 27 - 8 }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 21 \sqrt { 3 } + 2 \sqrt { 2 } } { 19 }{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 21 ( 1.732 ) + 2 ( 1.414 ) } { 19 }{/tex}

{tex}= \frac { 36.372 + 2.828 } { 19 } {/tex}
= 2.063

Q 4

If {tex}a = \frac{{3 + \sqrt 5 }}{2}{/tex}, then find the value of {tex}{a^2} + \frac{1}{{{a^2}}}{/tex}.

Solution

We have {tex}a = \frac{{3 + \sqrt 5 }}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \;{a^2} = \frac{{{{(3 + \sqrt 5 )}^2}}}{4}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{9 + 5 + 6\sqrt 5 }}{4} = \frac{{14 + 6\sqrt 5 }}{4} = \frac{{7 + 3\sqrt 5 }}{2}{/tex}
Now, {tex}\frac{1}{{{a^2}}} = \frac{2}{{7 + 3\sqrt 5 }} = \frac{2}{{7 + 3\sqrt 5 }} \times \frac{{7 - 3\sqrt 5 }}{{7 - 3\sqrt 5 }}{/tex}{tex}= \frac{{2(7 - 3\sqrt 5 )}}{{{{(7)}^2} - {{(3\sqrt 5 )}^2}}}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{2(7 - 3\sqrt 5 )}}{{49 - 45}} = \frac{{2(7 - 3\sqrt 5 )}}{4} = \frac{{7 - 3\sqrt 5 }}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore {a^2} + \frac{1}{{{a^2}}} = \frac{{7 + 3\sqrt 5 }}{2} + \frac{{7 - 3\sqrt 5 }}{2}{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{{7 + 3\sqrt 5 + 7 - 3\sqrt 5 }}{2} = \frac{{14}}{2} = 7{/tex}

Q 5

If {tex}x = \frac{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 }}{/tex}, and {tex}y = \frac{{\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{/tex}, then find the value of x2 + y2.

Solution

{tex}x = \frac{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 }} \times \frac{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{{\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 }}{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{{{{(\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 )}^2}}}{{{{(\sqrt 3 )}^2} - {{(\sqrt 2 )}^2}}} = \frac{{3 + 2 + 2\sqrt {3 \times 2} }}{{3 - 2}}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x = \frac{{5 + 2\sqrt 6 }}{1} = 5 + 2\sqrt 6{/tex}
Similarly, {tex}y = 5 - 2\sqrt 6{/tex}
Now, {tex}x + y = 5 + 2\sqrt 6 + 5 - 2\sqrt 6 = 10{/tex}
And, {tex}xy = \frac{{(\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 )}}{{(\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 )}} \times \frac{{(\sqrt 3 - \sqrt 2 )}}{{(\sqrt 3 + \sqrt 2 )}} = 1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x2 + y2 = (10)2 - 2(1)2 = 100 - 2 = 98

Q 6

Simplify: {tex}{(256)^{ - \left( {{4^{ - \frac{3}{2}}}} \right)}}{/tex}.

Solution

{tex}{(256)^{ - \left( {{4^{ - \,\frac{3}{2}}}} \right)}} = {\left( {{2^8}} \right)^{ - \left( {{4^{ - \,\frac{3}{2}}}} \right)}}{/tex}{tex} = {\left( {{2^8}} \right)^{ - \left( {{2^{2 \times - \,\frac{3}{2}}}} \right)}} = {\left( {{2^8}} \right)^{ - \left( {{2^{ - 3}}} \right)}}{/tex}
{tex} = {\left( {{2^8}} \right)^{ - \left( {\frac{1}{8}} \right)}} = {2^{8 \times \left( { - \frac{1}{8}} \right)}} = {2^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{2}{/tex}

Q 7

Find the value of {tex}\frac{4}{{{{(216)}^{ - \frac{2}{3}}}}} + \frac{1}{{{{(256)}^{ - \frac{3}{4}}}}} + \frac{2}{{{{(243)}^{ - \frac{1}{5}}}}}{/tex}

Solution

We have {tex}\frac{4}{{{{(216)}^{ - \frac{2}{3}}}}} + \frac{1}{{{{(256)}^{ - \frac{3}{4}}}}} + \frac{2}{{{{(243)}^{ - \frac{1}{5}}}}}{/tex}
{tex}= 4{(216)^{\frac{2}{3}}} + {(256)^{\frac{3}{4}}} + 2{(243)^{\frac{1}{5}}}{/tex}
{tex}= 4{({6^3})^{\frac{2}{3}}} + {({4^4})^{\frac{3}{4}}} + 2{({3^5})^{\frac{1}{5}}}{/tex}
{tex} = 4 \times {6^{3 \times \frac{2}{3}}} + {4^{4 \times \frac{3}{4}}} + 2 \times {3^{5 \times \frac{1}{5}}}{/tex}
{tex} = 4 \times {6^2} + {4^3} + 2 \times 3{/tex}
= 144 + 64 + 6 = 214

sections.html:
Q 1

Which of the following is not equal to {tex}\left[\left(\frac{5}{6}\right)^{\frac{1}{5}}\right]^{-\frac{1}{6}}{/tex}?

Q 2

Are there two irrational numbers whose sum and product both are rationals? Justify.

Solution

Yes.
consider {tex}\sqrt{2} \text { and } -\sqrt{2}{/tex}  as two irrational numbers.
{tex}\sqrt{2}-\sqrt{2}=0{/tex}, a rational number.
{tex}\sqrt{2} \times(-\sqrt{2})= -2{/tex}, a rational number

Thus, we have two irrational numbers whose sum and product both are rationals.

Q 3

State whether the following statement is true: There is a number x such that x2 is irrational but x4 is rational. Justify your answer by an example.

Solution

True.
Let us take {tex}x=\sqrt[4]{2}{/tex}
Now, {tex}x^{2}=(\sqrt[4]{2})^{2}=\sqrt{2},{/tex} an irrational number.
{tex}x^{4}=(\sqrt[4]{2})^{4}=2{/tex}, a rational number.
So, we have a number x such that x2 is irrational but x4 is rational.

Q 4

Locate {tex}\sqrt{13}{/tex} on the number line.

Solution

Here, 13 = 9 + 4 = 32 + 22  [sum of square of two natural numbers]
So, we take a = 3 and b = 2
Draw OA = 3 units on number line and then draw AB = 2 units, such that AB {tex}\perp{/tex} OA.
Join OB.

By using Pythagoras theorem, in {tex}\Delta{/tex}OAB
{tex}O B=\sqrt{O A^{2}+A B^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{3^{2}+2^{2}}{/tex}
{tex}=\sqrt{9+4}=\sqrt{13}{/tex}
Taking O as centre and radius equal to OB, draw an arc, which cuts the number line at C. Hence, OC represents {tex}\sqrt{13}{/tex}.

Q 5

Express {tex}0.12 \overline{3}{/tex} in the form {tex}\frac{p}{q}{/tex}, where p and q are integers and q {tex}\ne{/tex} 0.

Solution

Let {tex}x=0.12 \overline{3}{/tex}
i.e, x = 0.12333... 
multiply both sides by 100, we get,
100 x = 12.333... ---(i)
multiply eq. (i) by 10, we get,
1000 x = 123.333...----(ii)
subtracting eq. (i) from (ii)
1000 x - 100 x = 123.333... - 12.333...
900 x = 111
x = {tex}\frac{111}{900}{/tex}
Therefore, {tex}0.12 \overline{3}=\frac{111}{900}=\frac{37}{300}{/tex}

Q 6

Simplify: {tex}(3 \sqrt{5}-5 \sqrt{2})(4 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}){/tex}

Solution

{tex}(3 \sqrt{5}-5 \sqrt{2})(4 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}){/tex}

= {tex}3 \sqrt{5}{/tex}{tex}(4 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}){/tex} - {tex}5 \sqrt{2}{/tex}{tex}(4 \sqrt{5}+3 \sqrt{2}){/tex}

= {tex}12 \times 5 +{/tex} 9{tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex} - 20{tex}\sqrt{10}{/tex} - {tex}15 \times 2{/tex}
{tex}=60+9 \sqrt{10}-20 \sqrt{10}-30{/tex}
{tex}=30-11 \sqrt{10}{/tex}

Q 7

Find the value of a : {tex}\frac{6}{3 \sqrt{2}-2 \sqrt{3}}=3 \sqrt{2}-a \sqrt{3}{/tex}

Solution

 {tex}3 \sqrt{2}-a \sqrt{3} {/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{6}{3 \sqrt{2}-2 \sqrt{3}} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{6}{3 \sqrt{2}-2 \sqrt{3}} \times \frac{3 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{3}}{3 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{3}} {/tex}
={tex}\frac{6(3 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{3})}{(3 \sqrt{2})^{2}-(2 \sqrt{3})^{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{6(3 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{3})}{18-12} {/tex}
= {tex}\frac{6(3 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{3})}{6} {/tex}
={tex}3 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{3}{/tex} 
On comparing, 
{tex}3 \sqrt{2}-a \sqrt{3} {/tex}  = {tex}3 \sqrt{2}+2 \sqrt{3}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = -2

Q 8

Simplify: {tex}\left[5\left(8^{\frac{1}{3}}+27^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}

Solution

{tex}\left[5\left(8^{\frac{1}{3}}+27^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
 ={tex}\left[5\left(\left(2^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}+\left(3^{3}\right)^{\frac{1}{3}}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[5\left(\left(2\right)^{\frac{1}{3}\times 3}+\left(3\right)^{\frac{1}{3}\times3}\right)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
={tex}\left[5(2+3)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
={tex}\left[5(5)^{3}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
={tex}\left[5^{4}\right]^{\frac{1}{4}}{/tex}
= 5

Q 9

If a = 5 + 2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex} and b {tex}=\frac1a{/tex}, then what will be the value of a2 + b2?

Solution

a = 5 + 2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}
{tex}b=\frac{1}{a}=\frac{1}{5+2 \sqrt{6}}{/tex}{tex}=\frac{1}{5+2 \sqrt{6}} \times \frac{5-2 \sqrt{6}}{5-2 \sqrt{6}}{/tex} (Rationalise the denominator)
{tex}=\frac{5-2 \sqrt{6}}{5^{2}-(2 \sqrt{6})^{2}}{/tex}   [using (a+b)(a-b) = a2 - b2]

{tex}=\frac{5-2 \sqrt{6}}{25-24}=5-2 \sqrt{6}{/tex}
since  a2 + b2 = (a+b)2 - 2ab
Here, a + b = (5 + 2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}) + (5 - 2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}) = 10
ab = (5 + 2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex})(5 - 2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex}) = 52 - (2{tex}\sqrt{6}{/tex})2 = 25 - 24 = 1
Therefore, a2 + b2 = 102 - 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 = 100 - 2 = 98
